title: linaria reflexa subsp. agglutinans
intro: Linaria reflexa subsp. agglutinans, also known as the toadflax or butter and eggs, is a plant species that belongs to the family Plantaginaceae. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antispasmodic, and expectorant effects. The health benefits of Linaria reflexa subsp. agglutinans include its ability to treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its use as a natural diuretic to help with kidney problems. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, tinctures, or infusions, and it is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
